title: Methylmalonic Acid (MMA) Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
eening . A newborn screening checks for a variety of health conditions. An MMA test checks for methylmalonic acidemia , a rare genetic disorder . Symptoms of this disorder can range from mild to severe and may include:
Vomiting 
Dehydration 
Developmental delays 
Intellectual disability, which means that a person has trouble with intellectual functioning (such as learning and communicating) and everyday social and life skills. 
Why do I need an MMA test? 
You may need this test if you have symptoms of a vitamin B12 deficiency. These may include:
Fatigue 
Diarrhea 
Dizziness 
Shortness of breath 
Loss of appetite 
Tingling in hands and/or feet 
Mood changes 
Confusion 
Irritability 
Pale skin 
You might be at a higher risk for B12 deficiency if you:
Are a strict vegetarian or vegan 
Have a digestive disease , such as celiac disease Â or Crohn's disease 
Are a person with an alcohol use disorder Â (AUD) 
Are older than 75 years 
Have Sjogren's syndrome , a disease that causes dry eyes and mouth 
Have had certain types of stomach or intestinal surgery 
If you have a new baby, they will probably be tested as part of a newborn screening.
What happens during an MMA test? 
MMA levels may be checked in blood or urine.
During a blood test , a health care professional will take a blood sample from a vein in your arm, using a small needle. After the needle is inserted, a small amount of b
